dev.jeka.core.api.http

Class JkHttpRequest

  • java.lang.Object
    • dev.jeka.core.api.http.JkHttpRequest

  • public class JkHttpRequest
extends java.lang.Object
    A utility class for constructing and executing HTTP requests using HttpURLConnection. This class provides methods for configuring various aspects of the HTTP request such as headers, body, connection timeouts, and more. It also supports multipart file uploads.

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      static class  JkHttpRequest.MultipartPart

      Adds a header to the HTTP request.

      Adds a file as a multipart part to the HTTP request.

      Adds a file as a multipart part to the HTTP request.

      Adds a multipart part to the HTTP request.

      Consumes the response stream of an HTTP request, providing the status code and input stream to a specified BiConsumer.

      Applies a customizer function to the underlying HttpURLConnection.

      Downloads a file from the HTTP server and saves it to the specified file path.

      Executes the HTTP request configured for this HttpRequest instance and returns the corresponding HttpResponse.
      static JkHttpRequest of(java.lang.String url)
      Creates an instance of HttpRequest configured with the specified URL and defaults to the "GET" HTTP method.
      static JkHttpRequest of(java.lang.String url, java.lang.String method)
      Creates an instance of HttpRequest configured with the specified URL and HTTP method.
      static JkHttpRequest ofMultipart(java.lang.String url)
      Creates an instance of HttpRequest configured for a multipart/form-data POST request.

      Sets the body content for this HttpRequest.

      Sets the read timeout for the HTTP request.

      Sets the HTTP request method for this HttpRequest.

      Configures whether the underlying HttpURLConnection should use caches.

    • Method Detail

      • of

        public static JkHttpRequest of(java.lang.String url,
                               java.lang.String method)
        Creates an instance of HttpRequest configured with the specified URL and HTTP method.
        Parameters:
        url - the URL for the HTTP request.
        method - the HTTP method to be used for the request (e.g., "GET", "POST").
        Returns:
        a new HttpRequest object representing the HTTP request.
        Throws:
        java.io.UncheckedIOException - if an I/O error occurs while creating the connection.

      • of

        public static JkHttpRequest of(java.lang.String url)
        Creates an instance of HttpRequest configured with the specified URL and defaults to the "GET" HTTP method.
        Parameters:
        url - the URL for the HTTP request.
        Returns:
        a new HttpRequest object representing the HTTP request.
        Throws:
        java.io.UncheckedIOException - if an I/O error occurs while creating the connection.

      • ofMultipart

        public static JkHttpRequest ofMultipart(java.lang.String url)
        Creates an instance of HttpRequest configured for a multipart/form-data POST request.
        Parameters:
        url - the URL for the HTTP request.
        Returns:
        a new HttpRequest object configured for multipart/form-data handling.
        Throws:
        java.io.UncheckedIOException - if an I/O error occurs while creating the connection.

      • setRequestMethod

        public JkHttpRequest setRequestMethod(java.lang.String method)
        Sets the HTTP request method for this HttpRequest.
        Parameters:
        method - the HTTP method to be used for the request (e.g., "GET", "POST"). The provided method will be converted to upper case.
        Returns:
        this HttpRequest instance, allowing for method chaining.
        Throws:
        java.io.UncheckedIOException - if the specified method is invalid or an I/O error occurs.

      • addHeader

        public JkHttpRequest addHeader(java.lang.String name,
                               java.lang.String value)
        Adds a header to the HTTP request.
        Parameters:
        name - the name of the header to be added.
        value - the value of the header to be added.
        Returns:
        this HttpRequest instance, allowing for method chaining.

      • addHeaders

        public JkHttpRequest addHeaders(java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.String> headers)

      • addMultipartPart

        public JkHttpRequest addMultipartPart(JkHttpRequest.MultipartPart part)
        Adds a multipart part to the HTTP request. This method writes the content of the given MultipartPart to the underlying HttpURLConnection's output stream.
        Parameters:
        part - the JkHttpRequest.MultipartPart to be added to the HTTP request. The part must contain the necessary field name, file name, and file content to be included in the multipart request body.
        Returns:
        this HttpRequest instance, allowing for method chaining.
        Throws:
        java.io.UncheckedIOException - if an I/O error occurs while flushing the part's content to the stream.

      • addMultipartFile

        public JkHttpRequest addMultipartFile(java.lang.String fieldName,
                                      java.lang.String fileName,
                                      java.nio.file.Path file)
        Adds a file as a multipart part to the HTTP request. The file is included in the multipart request body with the specified field name and file name.
        Parameters:
        fieldName - the field name for the file in the multipart request.
        fileName - the name of the file to be sent in the multipart request.
        file - the Path representing the file to be added.
        Returns:
        this HttpRequest instance, allowing for method chaining.

      • addMultipartFile

        public JkHttpRequest addMultipartFile(java.lang.String fieldName,
                                      java.nio.file.Path file)
        Adds a file as a multipart part to the HTTP request. The file is included in the multipart request body with the specified field name and the file's name derived from the provided Path.
        Parameters:
        fieldName - the field name for the file in the multipart request.
        file - the Path representing the file to be added.
        Returns:
        this HttpRequest instance, allowing for method chaining.

      • setBody

        public JkHttpRequest setBody(java.lang.String body)
        Sets the body content for this HttpRequest. The provided body is written to the output stream of the underlying HttpURLConnection. The method enables output mode on the connection and ensures the body content is sent in the request.
        Parameters:
        body - the content to be used as the request body. The content is encoded as bytes using the platform's default charset.
        Returns:
        this HttpRequest instance, allowing for method chaining.
        Throws:
        java.io.UncheckedIOException - if an I/O error occurs while writing the body content to the output stream.

      • useCaches

        public JkHttpRequest useCaches(boolean useCaches)
        Configures whether the underlying HttpURLConnection should use caches.
        Parameters:
        useCaches - a boolean indicating whether caching is enabled for this connection. If true, the connection is allowed to use cached responses.
        Returns:
        this HttpRequest instance, allowing for method chaining.

      • setReadTimeout

        public JkHttpRequest setReadTimeout(int readTimeout)
        Sets the read timeout for the HTTP request. The read timeout is the amount of time, in milliseconds, to wait for a response from the server after the request has been sent.
        Parameters:
        readTimeout - the timeout value in milliseconds. A value of 0 means no timeout.
        Returns:
        this HttpRequest instance, allowing for method chaining.

      • setFollowRedirect

        public JkHttpRequest setFollowRedirect(boolean followRedirect)

      • setConnectionTimeout

        public JkHttpRequest setConnectionTimeout(int connectionTimeout)

      • setDoInput

        public JkHttpRequest setDoInput(boolean doInput)

      • setDoOutput

        public JkHttpRequest setDoOutput(boolean doOutput)

      • customize

        public JkHttpRequest customize(java.util.function.Consumer<java.net.HttpURLConnection> customizer)
        Applies a customizer function to the underlying HttpURLConnection. This allows for direct customization of the HttpURLConnection instance associated with this HttpRequest.
        Parameters:
        customizer - a Consumer that accepts the HttpURLConnection to be customized. The provided consumer can perform operations such as setting headers, configuring connection properties, or manipulating the connection.
        Returns:
        this HttpRequest instance, allowing for method chaining.

      • execute

        public JkHttpResponse execute()
        Executes the HTTP request configured for this HttpRequest instance and returns the corresponding HttpResponse.
        Returns:
        an HttpResponse object containing the status code, headers, and body of the response received from the server.

      • consumeResponseStream

        public int consumeResponseStream(java.util.function.BiConsumer<java.lang.Integer,java.io.InputStream> consumer)
        Consumes the response stream of an HTTP request, providing the status code and input stream to a specified BiConsumer. The method attempts to retrieve the response code and input stream from the underlying HTTP connection, and then applies the provided consumer to process them. The HTTP connection is disconnected after processing.
        Parameters:
        consumer - a BiConsumer that accepts the HTTP response status code and the input stream representing the response content. The consumer is responsible for handling the provided data.
        Returns:
        the HTTP response status code received from the server.
        Throws:
        java.io.UncheckedIOException - if an I/O error occurs while accessing the connection or input stream.

      • downloadFile

        public int downloadFile(java.nio.file.Path downloadFilePath)
        Downloads a file from the HTTP server and saves it to the specified file path.
        Parameters:
        downloadFilePath - the path where the downloaded file will be saved. The parent directories are created if they do not exist.
        Returns:
        the HTTP response status code from the server after the download attempt.
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the server responds with a status code indicating an error (HTTP 4xx or 5xx) or if there is no file to download at the specified URL.
